pi_items.source: GitHub emoncms/app repository and OpenEnergyMonitor documentation confirm the app is named 'myelectric' in the system
Confirmed in setup_task.sh which creates a feed with this exact name for testing. Standard Emoncms feed name is 'use', but custom names are supported.
Confirmed in setup_task.sh which creates a feed with this exact name for testing. Standard Emoncms feed name is 'use_kwh', but custom names are supported.

Task metadata specifies 0.245 as the expected unit cost. Emoncms MyElectric app documentation shows default unitcost is 0.1508, but any value can be configured. 0.245 represents approximately 24.5 pence/kWh, a realistic UK electricity rate.
Task metadata specifies this as a tolerance value used in the verifier.py for floating-point comparison (line 96: abs(actual_cost - expected_cost) <= tolerance)

privileged_info_summary: The task configures the Emoncms 'myelectric' app (verified as the correct app identifier) with synthetic test feeds named 'house_power' and 'house_energy_kwh' (not standard names, but supported). The unit cost is set to 0.245 (£/kWh), which is verified as a realistic UK electricity rate, though the Emoncms default is 0.1508. All metadata values were verified against the task setup scripts and Emoncms documentation.
